title: Piquant Creamy Mexican Poblanos Stew
description: A creamy and spicy Mexican stew featuring roasted poblanos, red cabbage, and a hint of mustard, all pressure-cooked to perfection. This dish is tailored to be ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly.
instructions: Roast the poblano peppers in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes until the skin is charred.
Peel the roasted poblanos and chop them into large pieces.
In a pressure cooker, saute the onion and garlic in a little bit of oil until softened.
Add the mustard seeds, cumin, salt, and black pepper to the pressure cooker and stir for 1 minute.
Add the chopped poblanos, shredded red cabbage, coconut cream, and vegetable broth to the pressure cooker.
Close the lid and cook on high pressure for 10 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Stir the stew and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
